rubric.evidence: Real in-browser run (headless Chrome, port-served, WASM q8): first visit showed Download (~279 MB), the real download progressed 28%→43%→74%→ready, then transformers.js text-classification produced live output. Console errors: []. Network failures: []. Model actually loaded via createModelLoader + Web Worker.
rubric.notes: Also verified in Node (transformers.js 3.7.5) as a runnability pre-check before building.

rubric.evidence: 3-class softmax, correctly labelled across languages: es 'Me encanta…😍' → Positive 93.3% (Neg 1.5 / Neu 5.1 / Pos 93.3, sums ~99.9%); en 'the worst experience…terrible' → Negative 94.7%; fr 'J'adore ce produit…' → Positive 93.8%. Labels map by name to negative/neutral/positive per config id2label.
rubric.notes: Semantic sanity confirmed on 3+ languages (≥2 required).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (onnx-community/twitter-xlm-roberta-base-sentiment-ONNX, linked), base model, task (text-classification), backend (WASM), dtype (q8 / model_quantized.onnx), size (~279 MB). Readout shows backend=WASM, latency=79 ms live. Inference off the main thread (worker.js, new Worker).

Two-tab test in one browser (shared Cache Storage + IndexedDB): tab1 downloaded → ready; tab2 revisit auto-initialised to state='ready' with NO Download button and the verdict rendered automatically (🙂 Positive 93.3%). 'Clear cached model' manage control present once ready. Uses shared createModelLoader — no hand-rolled Load button.

role=status aria-live loader region + a #runStatus live region; download progress shown as a percentage; errors surface on the page (.status.err) with a Retry path via the shared loader. Offline/partial/eviction handled by the shared model-cache layer (inherited, proven across 120 built demos).
rubric.notes: Offline + eviction paths are inherited from the shared loader; not re-driven on this page (manual-evidenced in conformance).

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and operable: textarea (debounced live scoring), 8 language sample chips (min-block-size 2.2rem tap target), 'Explain the last verdict' button (occlusion attribution → 7–9 word spans rendered). All keyboard reachable; buttons carry text labels.

No canned output. The verdict, probability bars, detected-language readout and word-attribution all derive from the on-device worker run; latency and backend are measured, not claimed. Removing the model would leave the panel empty, not faked.

Live regions announce status/verdict; emoji in verdict/bars are aria-hidden with a text label alongside; buttons have accessible names; no images without alt (none on page). Colour is paired with text labels (not colour-only). Contrast passes in both themes (see ran-light.png / ran-dark.png).
rubric.notes: Follow-up: confirm focus-visible order through the chip row and tooltip discoverability of the attribution log-odds on touch (title attr is hover-only).

rubric.evidence: responsive-check clean on desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740 DPR3 touch): no horizontal overflow, no clipped controls, console+network clean (reports/responsive/screens/multilingual-sentiment/{desktop,mobile}.png). Multi-model page stacks its 3-column stage grid to 1 column under 560px.
rubric.notes: Automated signal ⇒ support recorded needs-review; an agent read of the mobile screenshot confirms layout, but tap-target audit on the dense sample chips is a follow-up before flipping to ok.

rubric.evidence: Inference is entirely off the main thread (dedicated Web Worker); the control UI stays responsive during the ~279 MB download and during scoring. Occlusion attribution is capped at 40 words to bound the forward-pass count. Live scoring is debounced (350 ms).

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel layout without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox with flex-wrap:wrap, intrinsic sizing and logical properties; track overflow (§6) so narrow viewports never spill; minmax()/fr tracks over fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — sample rows use flex-wrap:wrap; probability rows use grid minmax(6.5rem,auto) 1fr auto with min-inline-size:0 on the bar track; multi-model stage grid collapses 1fr auto 1fr → 1 column under 560px. responsive-check confirms no horizontal overflow on both classes.

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long tasks keep INP low web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy synchronous work off the main thread / yield so input + repaint stay responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all model load + inference + occlusion attribution runs in a dedicated Web Worker (worker.js); the main thread only posts messages and renders. Attribution passes are capped at 40 words.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js new Worker; UI stayed responsive during the 279 MB download in the headless run

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status announcements loading progress
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an aria-live region; give every control an accessible name; don't rely on colour alone.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader uses role=status aria-live; #runStatus + #verdict + #probs are aria-live=polite; verdict/bars pair colour with an emoji + text label; decorative emoji are aria-hidden.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: DOM has [role=status][aria-live]; verdict text '🙂 Positive 93.3%' read from the live region in the headless run

openQuestions: The attribution tooltip (exact log-odds pull) uses the title attribute — hover-only. Should touch users get a tap-to-reveal affordance?
The base model has no formally specified licence — is 'unspecified / cite the papers' the right catalogue treatment, or should it be blocked pending a licence?
On very short emoji-only input the model is confident but occlusion attribution has little to remove — is a minimum-length hint worth adding to the wild page?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass: read the mobile screenshot for tap-target size on the dense language sample chip row and flip support desktop/mobile to ok (or unsupported+evidence).
Add a touch-friendly reveal for the per-word log-odds attribution (currently a hover title), so the see-inside surface works on mobile.
Additive wild demo: a live 'sarcasm vs literal' A/B where the user rewrites a post until the model flips, teaching where tweet-trained sentiment breaks.

summary: Revision-1 critique for multilingual-sentiment (onnx-community/twitter-xlm-roberta-base-sentiment-ONNX, text-classification, WASM q8), evidenced by a real headless-Chrome run: correct 3-class output across es/en/fr, cached-reload auto-init with no Download button, console+network clean, and clean responsive matrix on both classes. Strong across real-inference, io-shape, cache-init, controls and no-fake-output; minor follow-ups on mobile tap-target verification and a touch affordance for the attribution tooltip.
